candidate_problems.text: What precise criterion distinguishes a genuinely load-bearing agential explanation from a merely higher-level but dispensable summary of microphysics? The conjecture relies on indispensability, but it leaves open how to test this non-circularly across cases like humans, chess engines, manipulated agents, and institutions.

How can reasons be causally efficacious without either collapsing into microphysical causation or becoming a mysterious extra force? The conjecture posits layered causation, but the metaphysical and explanatory relation between physical instantiation and agential constraint remains unresolved.

Which structural features are necessary and sufficient for responsibility-grounding free will—reason-responsiveness, counterfactual representation, self-correction, autonomy from bypass/manipulation, or something else—and how do these features vary by degree across persons and systems? The conjecture gestures at these capacities but does not yet yield a principled boundary or metric.
